purpose: Classifies general-purpose technologies by whether they can be adopted with or without organizational restructuring
components: Plug-and-play GPTs: can be adopted within existing organizational structures (incremental efficiency gains)
Reorganization-dependent GPTs: require fundamental restructuring of work processes, management, and organizational design
Reorganization is 60-70% of total productivity lag historically
AI is reorganization-dependent but may partially self-accelerate Phase 2 (recursive reorganization)
current_assessment: AI is a reorganization-dependent GPT, which explains the long productivity lag. The key unresolvable question is whether AI's ability to assist in its own organizational restructuring compresses the historical timeline.
limitations: Binary classification may be too coarse — GPTs exist on a spectrum
Recursive reorganization hypothesis is untestable until it either happens or doesn't
Does not specify which organizations need what type of reorganization
Historical analogy base is small (4 GPTs)
